prompt: A technician suspects acid and moisture contamination in a recovered refrigerant stream. Which precaution best protects recovery equipment during evacuation?

options.text: Use only brass fittings on all service hoses
Skip recovery and vent the charge to atmosphere
Install an in-line filter drier ahead of the recovery machine inlet

explanation: Acid and moisture can damage recovery equipment and contaminate cylinders. An in-line filter drier before the recovery machine helps trap contaminants and protect the unit during recovery.

distractorExplanations.a: Fitting material selection does not replace contaminant filtration before the recovery machine.
distractorExplanations.b: Venting regulated refrigerant is prohibited under Section 608.
distractorExplanations.d: Heating cylinders does not address acid contamination and can create unsafe pressure conditions.

prompt: Which statement best describes EPA requirements before opening a small appliance refrigeration circuit for service?

options.text: Refrigerant must be recovered to required levels before opening the sealed system
Up to 2 pounds may be vented if the appliance is older than 10 years
Recovery is optional when the factory charge is under 5 pounds
Only Universal certification allows small-appliance service

explanation: Before opening a sealed refrigeration circuit, technicians must recover refrigerant to EPA-required levels using certified equipment; intentional venting is prohibited.

distractorExplanations.b: Age of equipment does not permit venting regulated refrigerant.
distractorExplanations.c: The 5-pound limit defines Type I equipment class, not an exemption from recovery before opening the circuit.
distractorExplanations.d: Type I certification covers small appliances; Universal is broader but not the only allowed credential.
